title: Fix sig-proxy=false test and use image cache
body:
Pulling fedora-minimal was potentially causing timeouts, which is bad. Using the cache avoids that.

Sig-proxy=false test was entirely nonfunctional - I think we didn't update it when we fixed sig-proxy=true to be less racy. It was still passing, which is concerning.

comments.body:
@mheon I'm [not re-running this cgroupv2 test](https://cirrus-ci.com/task/6505938795626496) since the `--sigproxy` test error appears especially bad:

```
[+0733s] output: e4456159d9fd
[+0733s] Running: /var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/libpod/bin/podman --storage-opt vfs.imagestore=/tmp/podman/imagecachedir --root /tmp/podman_test380482087/crio --runroot /tmp/podman_test380482087/crio-run --runtime /usr/bin/crun --conmon /usr/libexec/podman/conmon --cni-config-dir /etc/cni/net.d --cgroup-manager systemd --tmpdir /tmp/podman_test380482087 --events-backend file --storage-driver vfs kill -s 9 test2
[+0733s] SIGFPE: floating-point exception
[+0733s] PC=0x4b826a m=0 sigcode=0
[+0733s] 
```
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably #4118) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.

I'd forgotten about it. I'll get the comment fixed so we can land it.
